summary: 9 concrete products and services surfaced from launch, show, and EU-stack discussions.

items.name: Chert
items.source_story_title: Launch HN: Chert (YC P26) – Twilio for iMessage
items.summary: API for sending and receiving iMessages at scale.
items.why_included: HN comments focused on Apple throttling, line health, and scaling infrastructure for iMessage workflows.
items.evidence: Commenters debated rotating identities, line health checks, and the risk of unlimited blast volume claims.

items.name: DeepSeek Reasonix
items.source_story_title: DeepSeek reasonix, DeepSeek native coding agent with high caching and low cost
items.summary: Terminal coding agent tuned for DeepSeek cache efficiency.
items.why_included: The thread debated cache-friendly agent loops, context stability, and low-cost coding workflows.
items.evidence: Comments highlighted append-only prompts, 90%+ cache-hit claims, and schema-aware tool-call repair.

items.name: Geomatic
items.source_story_title: Show HN: Geomatic – A command-driven geometry studio enabled with autodiff
items.summary: Command-driven geometry studio with autodiff and reactive geometry.
items.why_included: Commenters surfaced onboarding fixes and a concrete example bug, showing active early adoption.
items.evidence: One commenter linked a better getting-started page; another reported an unknown command: n-star bug.

items.name: White Rabbit
items.source_story_title: White Rabbit – sub-nanosecond synchronization for large distributed systems
items.summary: Hardware timing system for sub-nanosecond distributed synchronization.
items.why_included: The thread centered on real timing hardware, measured precision, and systems-level impact.
items.evidence: Comments cited ~10 ps jitter over 50 km fiber and pointed readers to the White Rabbit synchronization docs.

items.name: Audiomass
items.source_story_title: Show HN: Audiomass – a free, open-source multitrack audio editor for the web
items.summary: Free browser audio editor with multitrack editing.
items.why_included: Commenters praised the fast web UI and offline/PWA mode, with strong comparisons to desktop tools.
items.evidence: Users called it great, said they were dreading Audacity, and highlighted offline mode and multitrack editing.

items.name: Unikraft
items.source_story_title: The bootstrapper's EU stack for under €10 per month
items.summary: EU-hosted compute platform with free instances and Docker deploys.
items.why_included: A commenter singled it out as an unusually strong cheap compute option for EU-based bootstrappers.
items.evidence: The thread praised 2 free instances, EU hosting, and deploys via Dockerfile or Docker Compose.

items.name: Hetzner Cloud
items.source_story_title: The bootstrapper's EU stack for under €10 per month
items.summary: Low-cost EU cloud hosting for bootstrapped services.
items.why_included: Multiple commenters treated Hetzner as a reliable default for local-first EU infrastructure.
items.evidence: One commenter listed Hetzner Cloud, Box, and Object Storage as long-time services they happily recommend.

items.name: Brevo
items.source_story_title: The bootstrapper's EU stack for under €10 per month
items.summary: Transactional email service recommended after SendGrid's free-plan changes.
items.why_included: The thread gave it direct praise as a practical replacement for SendGrid.
items.evidence: Commenters said they moved from SendGrid to Brevo and saw solid deliverability on Google and Microsoft 365.

items.name: UpDown.io
items.source_story_title: The bootstrapper's EU stack for under €10 per month
items.summary: Monitoring service recommended as a dependable stack addition.
items.why_included: A commenter explicitly called it out as their preferred monitoring service.
items.evidence: The recommendation said UpDown.io was the monitor they use and recommend, even though it was missing from the original list.
